message:
refactor(config): guard WAL quarantine on is_file + replace shelled-out sqlite3 test setup

Two unrelated but mechanical changes to src/config/mod.rs:

1. `quarantine_truncated_wal_sidecar` used `fs::metadata(&wal_path).len()`
   after only an existence check. If `-wal` happened to be a directory
   (the WAL sidecar has been "stolen" by a directory of the same name
   after a botched rollback, or a user mkdir'd it manually), calling
   `.len()` on directory metadata gives filesystem-defined garbage and
   the `< 32` branch would then try to quarantine a directory, which
   later `fs::rename` would either fail on or do something surprising.

   Add a `meta.is_file()` guard right after the metadata lookup so we
   only quarantine regular-file WAL sidecars. Anything else is left
   alone for higher-level cleanup to handle.

2. Two malformed-db fixture helpers — `create_malformed_blocked_cache_db`
   and `insert_duplicate_issue_prefix_config_row` — used to shell out
   to the system `sqlite3` CLI with `Command::new("sqlite3")` in the
   test module. That was fragile (required sqlite3 on PATH at the
   exact version the project expects, test failure mode was
   stderr-as-string), and it bypassed the project's own fsqlite
   bindings entirely.

   Replace both with direct `fsqlite::Connection` usage:
   - `create_malformed_blocked_cache_db` now uses
     `crate::storage::schema::execute_batch` with the `CREATE TABLE`,
     `PRAGMA writable_schema=ON`, the duplicate `sqlite_master`
     insert, and an explicit `PRAGMA writable_schema=OFF` — the last
     of those was silently missing from the shell invocation, so the
     old path left `writable_schema` ON for the rest of the setup.
   - `insert_duplicate_issue_prefix_config_row` now opens a
     `Connection` and runs `execute(...)` with the same single-quote
     escaping logic.
   - Drop the now-unused `use std::process::Command;` at the top of
     the test module; replaced by `use fsqlite::Connection;`.

   Behaviour equivalent on the test fixtures we still exercise,
   but the test no longer needs an external binary and doesn't
   leave writable_schema ON inside the fixture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
